item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 my first upvote after signing up!

Unlike a couple of comments, I like the fact that the process shows up out of nowhere.

She reminds the researchers that it's probably their fault. They didn't think anything of it, and just filed it away as useless information.

It's a nice little dig at the foundation's idea of science and study.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This. This is my headcanon about crosstesting.
This is why I frown so much at them. XD

Oink!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sure, and risk the death tiggering another change?
The thing dies with every change, killing it may not be exactly the best choice here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Audibly said "What the fuck"

Uh, good job I guess, that was horrifying? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel that the whole porcine thing comes out of nowhere. Maybe add some some emphasising about how they tried to test her psychic powers on a pig that too had anomalous properties superficially similar to what happened later to see if she had the same effect on it as she had on humans?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the idea of a constantly mutating, escalating anomaly, and most of what this does is really good. But I'm downvoting based on the first entry (the listing for the original Jessica Lambert).

The first entry doesn't really fit together. Lambert has a very minor, low threat psychic power, so why does she need two guards at all times? Why is she fed twice a day with her very own nutrition chart, and why in gods name is she kept in a cube? This is a teenage girl, why would she ever be kept in a 5m high room? Being a cube doesn't make it a better cell. Boy do I hate cubes.
All of the following:

SCP-3127, currently located at Site-43, is to be contained in a 5m x 5m x 5m chamber and fed twice a day in accordance with Nutritional Chart 3127-1. SCP-3127's containment chamber is to be kept under guard by two security personnel at all times.

Could be easily replaced with:

SCP-3127, currently located at Site-43, is to be contained in a standard humanoid containment cell.

The first amendment feels weird. It comes across as a bit "Hey guys, let's throw some anomalous stuff at this lass. That'll be a lark." It feels like it's just there to allow for the later parts of the article (and yes, I know that that's true, but it shouldn't feel like that in world as well). I think it needs some stated purpose to the cross testing, probably just a sentence about testing to see if her emotional control effects could be duplicated and used to aid in containment of other anomalies; or something like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is amazing. I love it when an article starts with an apparently mundane entity and then surprises you with how original it can become. I was genuinely taken aback by the grotesque evolution of this thing. I hate to say so, but the "oink" at the end threatens to almost undo the integrity of the character and the mastery of the rest of the article as a horror piece. It feels like a really ill-placed grab at a bit of humorous punctuation. Imo of course. +1 anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For the love of god kill it before it gets any bigger. It's an uncontainable Keter that keeps growing. The last time you tried to contain it, it absorbed the entire facility. If this somehow isn't an End-of-the-world scenario in-progress, I don't know what is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the fuckiting hell +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's been three months and I'm still head-over-heels in love with this one. It's probably in my top 5, honestly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Me at first: Okay, it's fine….

Me halfway through the article: HAHAHAHAHAHA

Me by the end of the article: Ha. Ha. Ha….? Oh god that's creepy
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it. I think what makes it work is the way each iteration not only becomes worse, but way weirder as well. There's a harsh lesson against cross-testing in here, made even worse by the fact that the poor psychic girl was the one who sought out the authorities. Everything bad that happens to her is 100% the Foundation's fault. That moment when you accidentally turn an innocent teenage girl into a psychic screaming pig-Kaiju.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No joke, my favorite part.

item.title: Format Error
item.content:encoded: In UPDATE 02/02/2017 there's two Addendum 3127-1s.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Started out as a girl with mild psychic powers, ended up as a perpetually-screaming, building-sized pig that communicates in Morse code. Bravo!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The cold and procedure-adhering "[sic]" placed in the middle of her cry for help is the understated best part of this piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great. The iterative WTF kept me reading til the end.
+1

Random Nitpick: The 5 meter cube containment cell seemed a bit weird though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was profoundly unsettling.

Oink +1

item.title: what the fuck
item.content:encoded:
"constantly screaming" is where I got hooked.

+1, but with a trembling finger as I click the +.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry. Please please PLEASE do something. I feel like Im [sic] not even me anymore and I'm not even sure if I'm wrong about that. Sometimes I forget I'm not a pig.

Holy shit that line instantly got my +1. It's not very often that we get a glimpse of what the SCP is thinking of in these articles, and seeing just how scared this girl is and seeing how twisted each new instance is and knowing she's aware of what's going on every time it happens…. It's horrifying and I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed, thank you for pointing that out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This state lasts for up to fifty to sixty minutes

I think there's a bit of a grammar disconnect there. "Up to" is fine on its own, and so is "fifty to sixty minutes". Both together… not so much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Existential terror and body horror - two great tastes that taste great together!

I am so glad I wasn't planning on pork for dinner tonight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is quite a nightmarish fate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a trip.

In place of internal organs, SCP-3127's body contains a large number of organisms resembling miniature versions of Jessica Lambert, all of which are constantly screaming.

Like, holy fuck, what?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is absolutely my jam. Love the weird, love the body horror, love that the iterative documents don't feel tedious. Rock on. +1

This is why crosstesting is a bad idea, kids.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my gosh, that poor girl. Right in the feels. +1

Edit: Though TBH I am a bit sick of SCPs With Very Long and Unnecessarily Descriptive Titles. "Jessica Lambert" would've been fine. Not a big deal though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like that it's inexplicable. Too many skips these days have complex backstories that beat you over the head with their cleverness. I like that it was viscerally disturbing enough of body horror to make even a hardened genre fanatic like me shudder. I like the 'see? this is why we don't fuckin' cross-test' origin, too.

Def +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a pig in a pig-man hybrid in a pig in a human in a pig in a human in the hole at the bottom of the sea.

Nicely done. Goes from slightly funny to offputting rather quickly.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's been a while since I've seen an object that pulls off the escalating-WTF factor so effectively, to me at least.

Each of Jessica's iterations made my brows rise exponentially higher, and the lack of story is made up for by… just… the sheer fucking weirdness. I love every part of this, especially how it ended. I saw it coming, but I'm very curious how Jessica could possibly top herself. I imagine she might go back to Euclid, since her human forms seem consistently human-sized, but her screams transform people who hear them into domestic pigs the size of Research Installations.

Or a Kaiju-sized Jessica Lambert, forever cursed to wander the Earth with the power to scream buildings (and everyone in them) into pigs. Or pigs into buildings. Or people into pigs with buildings for organs… Oink.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This went from 'ehh sure I guess' to 'hahaha what the fuck?' very fast.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After reading this through a few times, I've decided to turn that -1 into a +1. This is a horrifically morbid article, but I still feel that the character development needs a few tweaks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The escalating effects and the twists it take on remind me of both SCP-3900 and SCP-3963 - but what distinguishes this one are the sheer morbidity and unexpected nature of the twist. The morse code ending is a bit trite, but it wasn't enough to dispel my sheer baffled amazement by the rest of the piece, which is incredibly dark and incredibly hilarious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a great read. Very imaginative and beyond disturbing. Oink.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Thanks to Westrin, Gummy_Dragon, XilasCrowe, Sindicate, chrisc99, not_a_seagull, Malcious and Ihpkmn for reading through this in chat!
